title: string codepoint model + bounded len
manager: FSL strings are sequences of Unicode codepoints, not UTF-16 code units, so `length` and indexing mean the human-obvious thing instead of splitting an emoji in half — the bug that quietly plagues JavaScript string code everywhere. Strings can also carry a bounded length, which drops them into the finite band, so a machine working over a bounded-string alphabet stays model-checkable instead of exploding to infinity the moment text is involved.
cs: codepoint (scalar value) model, not UTF-16 units; optional bounded length -> finite band; grapheme-aware ops in W6.57; normalization pinned in W6.58.
